text: What is the center of an atom?
fact: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons.

text: What planet is closest to the Sun?
fact: Mercury is the smallest planet too.

text: What gas do plants need for photosynthesis?
fact: Plants breathe it in.

text: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
fact: It produces energy.

text: How many continents are there?
fact: Asia, Africa, NA, SA, Antarctica, Europe, Australia.

text: What is the fastest land animal?
fact: It can reach 120 km/h.

text: What is the only mammal that can fly?
fact: Bats are mammals with wings.

text: Which planet has rings made of ice and dust?
fact: Saturn's rings are very famous.

text: What do bees collect from flowers?
fact: They turn nectar into honey.

text: What is the freezing point of water?
fact: At 0°C, water turns to ice.

text: What is the largest organ in the human body?
fact: Skin protects everything inside!

text: What force pulls objects toward the Earth?
fact: Gravity keeps us on the ground.

text: What is the closest planet to Earth?
fact: Venus is often called Earth's twin.

text: How long does it take Earth to orbit the Sun?
fact: It specifically takes 365.25 days.

text: What do you call a scientist who studies stars?
fact: Astronomers use telescopes.

text: What part of the plant makes food?
fact: Leaves use sunlight to make food.

text: Which animal is known as the 'King of the Jungle'?
fact: Actually, lions usually live in the savannah.

text: What is the hardest mineral?
fact: Only another diamond can scratch a diamond.

text: How many teeth does an adult human normally have?
fact: This includes the wisdom teeth.

text: What is the study of living things called?
fact: Bio means life.

text: What is the primary source of energy for Earth?
fact: The Sun provides light and heat.

text: What is the chemical symbol for water?
fact: Two hydrogen and one oxygen.

text: Which planet is the 'Red Planet'?
fact: Mars has reddish soil.

text: What do caterpillars turn into?
fact: It's called metamorphosis.

text: What is the tallest grass in the world?
fact: Bamboo can grow very fast!

text: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
fact: The Pacific covers 1/3 of Earth.

text: What gas do we breathe out?
fact: Plants breathe this in!

text: How many legs does a ladybug have?
fact: All insects have 6 legs.

text: What is the name of the galaxy we live in?
fact: It looks like milk in the sky.

text: What is a baby frog called?
fact: Tadpoles live in water.

text: What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs?
fact: A clock has hands and a face!

text: What has a thumb and four fingers but is not alive?
fact: Gloves fit our hands perfectly.

text: Which is heavier: a pound of feathers or a pound of lead?
fact: A pound is a pound!

text: What has to be broken before you can use it?
fact: You have to crack an egg to cook it.

text: I’m tall when I’m young, and I’m short when I’m old. What am I?
fact: Candles melt down as they burn.

text: What month of the year has 28 days?
fact: Every month has at least 28 days!

text: What is full of holes but still holds water?
fact: Sponges are very absorbent.

text: What building has the most stories?
fact: A library has thousands of stories (books)!

text: If you feed me, I live. If you give me a drink, I die. What am I?
fact: Water puts out fire.

text: What can you catch but not throw?
fact: You catch a cold (sickness)!

text: Logic Puzzle #11: If you have 3 apples and you take away 2, how many apples do you HAVE?
fact: You have the 2 apples you took!
